checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=4381732645.52 (2010-05-31) reverts to 2154950481.41 (2010-08-31), a 50.8% drop in one quarter

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=672236125.02 (2009-08-31) -> 765752844.02 (2011-02-28), modest ~14% growth over 18 months, roughly in line with balance sheet growth

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=6893130910.96 / SalesRevenueNet=14233515695.42 = 48.4% margin (FY2010, 2009-09-01/2010-08-31), a 9.3pp compression vs FY2009 despite only ~10% revenue decline

InventoryNet=3976493529.84 (2009-08-31) rising to 4824920574.72 (2009-11-30) then falling to 3712207150.04 (2010-08-31) - seasonal pattern generally consistent with revenue seasonality, but the margin compression noted above is inconsistent with stable inventory turns

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss ranges from -315788340.99 (2009-06-01/2009-08-31) to 1480003726.85 (2008-12-01/2009-02-28), tracking strong revenue seasonality (SalesRevenueNet peaks in Dec-Feb quarters, e.g. 5468695089.6 and 5272174448.22)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=2108869779.29 (2009-08-31) growing to 3221583203.96 (2011-02-28), implying allowance/AR ratio fell from ~10.4% to ~4.7% over the same window

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 8-K filed 2011-04-08 (accession 0000950123-11-034037) and a second 8-K filed the same date (accession 0000950123-11-034042) both containing recast multi-year (FY2008-FY2010) financial statement data, filed one day after the 10-Q (2011-04-07, accession 0000950123-11-033772)
8-K/A filed 2010-08-31 and 8-K/A filed 2010-04-07, indicating amendments to previously filed 8-Ks in the same reporting window

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data available from FY2008 (2007-09-01) through Q2 FY2011 (2011-02-28) across Assets, Revenue, NetIncomeLoss, CashFlow, AR, Inventory and reserve accounts, providing ~3.5 years of trend data
Some concepts (e.g., ShortTermInvestments, DebtCurrent, LongTermDebtCurrent) have only sparse/single data points limiting trend analysis for those specific items

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible premature revenue recognition, channel stuffing, or a receivable reclassification/timing shift causing a sharp one-quarter spike in receivables disconnected from revenue trend, which then reversed the following quarter

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=4381732645.52 (2010-05-31) vs 2836673809.8 (2010-02-28), a 54.5% jump while SalesRevenueNet fell from 5272174448.22 to 4014442343.35 over the same quarter
AccountsReceivableNetCurrent reverted to 2154950481.41 by 2010-08-31, a 50.8% decline in the subsequent quarter

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of bad-debt reserve as a percentage of receivables (reserve release) could be understating current-period bad debt/allowance expense and thereby overstating net income and receivable quality

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=219560992.45 (2009-08-31) declining to 150439939.27 (2011-02-28) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew from 2108869779.29 to 3221583203.96 over the same window, cutting the reserve ratio roughly in half

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible shift in cost/markdown recognition timing or reclassification between COGS and operating expenses contributing to a large gross-margin swing not proportionate to the revenue decline

rationale_evidence.quote: GrossProfit/SalesRevenueNet = 57.7% (FY2009: GrossProfit=9164638462.43, SalesRevenueNet=15889710342.14) vs 48.4% (FY2010: GrossProfit=6893130910.96, SalesRevenueNet=14233515695.42), a 9.3 percentage point compression against only a ~10% revenue decline

top_signals: Sharp one-quarter receivables spike (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent +54.5% while revenue fell 23.9%) that fully reversed the next quarter
Allowance for doubtful accounts falling from ~10.4% to ~4.7% of receivables even as receivables grew
Gross margin compression of ~9.3pp between FY2009 and FY2010 not proportionate to revenue decline
Two same-day 8-Ks (2011-04-08) recasting multi-year financials filed immediately after the 10-Q, alongside 8-K/A amendments, raising minor chronology irregularity concerns
